title: Effects of Cryogenic Rolling on the Microstructure Evolution and Mechanical Properties of Low-Density Fe-28Mn-8Al-1C Steel
abstract_summary: This study investigates the influence of cryogenic rolling (CR) deformation on the microstructure and mechanical properties of low-density Fe-28Mn-8Al-1C austenitic steel. Microstructural evolution involves transformations from equiaxed austenite grains to elongated grains with strain bands, twinning, dislocation entanglements, and dislocation cells, with texture changing from brass to copper with increasing deformation. Mechanical properties correlate directly with deformation extent: ultimate tensile strength, yield strength, and microhardness increase markedly, while elongation drops from 64% to 6.6%, revealing a strength–ductility trade-off. The findings demonstrate the potential of CR to tailor microstructure and properties of low-density steels.
research_objective: To comprehensively examine the effect of cryogenic rolling on the microstructure evolution and mechanical properties of low-density Fe-28Mn-8Al-1C steel, addressing its low yield strength and providing experimental evidence for achieving excellent comprehensive mechanical properties.
main_process_variables: cryogenic rolling with cumulative thickness reductions of 30%, 50%, 70%, 90%
solution treatment at 1000°C for 1 h followed by water quenching
liquid nitrogen immersion for 3–5 min before each pass
tensile testing for strength and elongation
key_sections: Introduction: Reviews low-density Fe-Mn-Al-C steel background, cold rolling limitations, cryogenic rolling advantages, and defines the research objective.
Experimental Section: Details material preparation, solution treatment, cryogenic rolling procedure with liquid nitrogen cooling, and deformation levels achieved.
Results: Reports microstructure evolution (grain morphology, dislocations, twinning, texture) and mechanical properties (engineering stress–strain curves, UTS, YS, elongation, microhardness).
Discussion: Explains strengthening mechanisms (dislocation strengthening, grain refinement), strength–ductility trade-off, and dislocation behavior.
Conclusion: Summarizes the main findings on microstructure–property relationships under cryogenic rolling.
